intro: Standard potential-outcome framework for econometric causal inference: PO systems, variables and bundles, consistency, counterfactual distributions, and the conditional-independence assumptions used in identification.

namespace_intros.Analysis: Analytic readings of potential outcomes: distributional (quantile) functionals, regression-function predicates, and population residualization primitives.
namespace_intros.Assumptions: The standard identification assumptions as predicates on a potential-outcome system: unconfoundedness, overlap, and their conditional refinements.
namespace_intros.Bridge: Bridges into the potential-outcome framework: the PO system induced by a structural causal model and by finite-coordinate restriction.
namespace_intros.Conditioning: Conditioning machinery for potential outcomes: σ-algebras generated by regimed variables and reusable event-level conditional-expectation tooling.
namespace_intros.Core: The graph-free potential-outcome core: systems, variables, finite intervention regimes, and joint counterfactual distributions.
namespace_intros.ID: Identification theory in the potential-outcome language: exact identification results and partial-identification (bounds) machinery.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact: Exact (point) identification: back-door and front-door adjustment, IV/LATE, regression discontinuity, proximal inference, dynamic regimes, and related theorems.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.QTE: Quantile treatment effects (Firpo 2007): backdoor identification of the marginal QTE at level τ — the τ-quantile of the treated potential outcome Y(1) minus that of the control Y(0). DistributionalBackdoor lifts the mean-level backdoor to the whole potential-outcome law via inverse-probability weighting; QuantileEffect adds the quantile layer, identifying the QTE as the difference of the identified laws quantiles.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.VariableIntensityIV: Variable-intensity instrumental variables: Angrist-Imbens average causal response over ordered treatment margins.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.DTR: Identification for dynamic treatment regimes: sequential exchangeability and the g-formula.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.DynamicLATE: Dynamic LATE: identification of complier effects in multi-period instrumented settings.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.HeckmanRoy: The Heckman/Roy selection model: identification of treatment parameters under self-selection.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.MultipleInstrumentIV: Multiple-instrument IV: Mogstad-Torgovitsky-Walters finite-support 2SLS response-type algebra and sign-alignment results.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.PartialLinear: Partially linear continuous-treatment identification: the potential-outcome setup, homogeneous dose-response restriction, Robinson residualization, and slope-identification theorem.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.Proximal: Proximal causal inference: identification through outcome- and treatment-confounding proxies via bridge functions.
namespace_intros.ID.Exact.RDD: Regression discontinuity: identification of the cutoff effect from continuity of conditional regressions.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial: Partial identification: bounds on treatment effects when point identification fails — Manski-style worst-case bounds, IV bounds, selection bounds, proxies, and the supporting set-valued machinery.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.BalkePearl: Balke-Pearl linear-programming bounds for the ATE under an imperfect instrument.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.CriterionSet: Identified sets defined by moment criteria: the criterion-function representation of partial identification.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Inference: Inference on partially identified parameters: confidence statements for identified sets.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.LP: The linear-programming representation of bound computations over finite outcome spaces.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Lee: Lee bounds: trimming bounds on treatment effects under sample selection with monotone response.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Manski: Manski worst-case and monotonicity-based bounds on treatment effects under bounded outcomes.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Proxy: Partial identification with proxy variables: sensitivity of bounds to proxy quality.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Proxy.Helpers: Supporting lemmas for the proxy-based partial-identification results.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Proxy.ZBased: Instrument-side (Z-based) proxy constructions for partial identification.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.RandomSet: Random-set foundations for partial identification: selections, Aumann expectations, and sharp identified sets.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Sensitivity: Sensitivity-analysis bounds for potential outcomes, including marginal-sensitivity-model feasible propensities, calibrated cutoffs, and treatment-effect intervals.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.Sensitivity.MSM: Marginal Sensitivity Model bounds: treated/control-arm feasible weights, cutoff construction, calibrated sharp upper and lower bounds, and ATE interval assembly.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.SupportFunction: Support-function representations of convex identified sets and their estimation theory.
namespace_intros.ID.Partial.SupportFunction.Sensitivity: Sensitivity analysis: worst-case reweighted mean over an ambiguity budget (general robust bounds + the χ²/L² ball), an applications layer of the support-function engine.
